---
title: "Work"
format: html
---

## Reflecting on Decisions: Work

By paying more attention to the decision making process, you will have a greater chance to interrupt the chain of decisions that could lead to a relapse. This is important because it is much easier to stop the process early, before you wind up in a high-risk situation, than later when you are in a situation that is harder to handle and may expose you to a number of triggers. Some times these decisions do not seem directly relevant to drinking, but nonetheless move one closer to drinking. <!--reference irrelevant_decisions.qmd-->


::: callout
### Reflect on the following questions:

- What situations at work tend to increase your stress, frustration, or urge to drink?

- Are there any work-related decisions or habits that may unintentionally put you at greater risk (e.g., skipping breaks, working late, isolating from coworkers, going to happy hours)?

- What warning signs tell you that work stress may be building toward a risky situation?

- What coping strategies could you use during or after work instead of drinking?

- What changes could you make to your work routine or schedule that would better support your recovery goals?
:::

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/workers.jpg?raw=true){fig-align="center"}
